perturbation.original_text: The Recipient will hold the Confidential Information in confidence and will not disclose the Confidential Information to any person or entity without the prior written consent of the Owner.
perturbation.changed_text: The Freelancer will hold the Confidential Information in confidence and will not disclose the Confidential Information to any person or entity without the prior written consent of the Owner.
perturbation.explanation: In Section II A., changing 'Recipient' to 'Freelancer' creates an ambiguity. 'Recipient' is the defined term, but suddenly the agreement refers to the 'Freelancer'. It is unclear if these terms are interchangable, contradicting the definition and weakening the obligations of confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Non Disclosure Agreement (this "Agreement" ) is made effective as of ________________, (the "Effective Date" ), by and between Jenna Marie Streitenfeld (the "Owner" ), of Kiss This Makeup, LLC located at 2342 Vintage DR, Lighthouse Point , FL 33064 , ________________ ____________________________________________ _ (the "Recipient" ), of _________________, _________________, ________ , _________________ (address of "Recipient"). The Owner is the President/CEO engaged in building professional relationships, maintaining a clientele, and providing beauty services in all multi media. The Recipient is a freelancer makeup artist or hair stylist providing their services for Kiss This Makeup, LLC. Information will be disclosed to the recipient in order to provide the best quali ty of service. The Owner has requested that the Recipient will protect the confidential material and information which may be disclosed between the Owner and the Recipient.
perturbation.justification: The original text uses 'Recipient' which is a defined term in the contradicted text as '________________ ____________________________________________ _ (the "Recipient" )'. The changed text replaces 'Recipient' with 'Freelancer', which is a different term and it isn't made clear that these terms are interchangeable. This creates an ambiguity and contradicts the defined term 'Recipient' in the contradicted text, leading to potential confusion about who the obligations apply to, creating a strong in-text contradiction according to the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition, since it uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them.

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient shall promptly advise the Owner if the Recipient becomes aware of any possible unauthorized disclosure or use of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: The Associate shall promptly advise the Owner if the Recipient becomes aware of any possible unauthorized disclosure or use of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Recipient' to 'Associate' in Section II. C creates confusion as to who is responsible for reporting unauthorized disclosures. Since 'Recipient' is still used later in the same sentence ('...if the Recipient becomes aware...'), it introduces ambiguity. Is the 'Associate' someone different from the 'Recipient'? This affects the enforcement of the reporting obligation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Associate shall promptly advise the Owner if the Recipient becomes aware of any possible unauthorized disclosure or use of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Recipient' to 'Associate' in the Changed Text creates a contradiction with the Contradicted Text, as both terms now appear in the same sentence, causing confusion about who is responsible for reporting unauthorized disclosures. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology' definition, which states that contradictions arise when the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations. The Explanation also states that confusion arises as to whether the 'Associate' is someone different from the 'Recipient'. The Contradicted text is a copy of the changed text, indicating that a contradiction was introduced due to the term changing. This is a strong contradiction because it affects the enforcement of the reporting obligation.

perturbation.original_text: Upon the written request of the Owner, the Recipient shall return to the Owner all written materials containing the Confidential Information. The Recipient shall also deliver to the Owner written statements signed by the Recipient certifying that all materials have been returned within five (5) days of receipt of the request.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon the written request of the Owner, the Freelancer shall return to the Owner all written materials containing the Confidential Information. The Recipient shall also deliver to the Owner written statements signed by the Recipient certifying that all materials have been returned within five (5) days of receipt of the request.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Recipient' to 'Freelancer' in Section V creates confusion. The Freelancer is now responsible for returning the materials, but then the Recipient must certify they were returned. It introduces uncertainty about who has the ultimate responsibility for returning the confidential information and certifying its return, affecting the enforceability of the return obligation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon the written request of the Owner, the Freelancer shall return to the Owner all written materials containing the Confidential Information. The Recipient shall also deliver to the Owner written statements signed by the Recipient certifying that all materials have been returned within five (5) days of receipt of the request.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Recipient' with 'Freelancer' in the first part of the sentence while retaining 'Recipient' in the second part. This creates a contradiction because it's unclear whether the Freelancer or the Recipient is responsible for providing the signed certification. This directly contradicts itself, by assigning the return of materials to 'Freelancer' but assigning the certification of return to the 'Recipient', even within the same sentence. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' because the terms 'Freelancer' and 'Recipient' are used in a conflicting manner, leading to uncertainty about who has the responsibility, impacting the enforceability of the return obligation. The contradicted text is in the exact same location which enhances the in-text contradiction.
